labels.name: devin:auto
labels.description: Dispatch trigger: adding this label sends the issue to the remediation fleet

labels.name: devin:queued
labels.description: Fleet state: waiting for a dispatch slot

labels.name: devin:in-progress
labels.description: Fleet state: a Devin session is working on this

labels.name: devin:pr-opened
labels.description: Fleet state: Devin opened a PR, awaiting human review

labels.name: devin:needs-human
labels.description: Fleet state: escalated to a human owner

labels.name: risk:low
labels.description: Low-risk change: eligible for automated remediation

labels.name: risk:medium
labels.description: Medium-risk change: automated with mandatory review

labels.name: risk:high
labels.description: High-risk change: excluded from automation by policy

issues.title: fix(py): replace deprecated datetime.utcnow() in superset/utils/cache.py
issues.body:
## Context
Python 3.12 deprecates `datetime.utcnow()`. This repo still has 27 call sites across 6 files; this issue covers one file.

## Target
- File: `superset/utils/cache.py` (2 call sites, lines ~76 and ~229)
- Scope: this file only. Do not touch other files.

## Required behavior
The codebase stores and compares **naive UTC** datetimes. A bare `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` returns an *aware* datetime and changes comparison semantics. Preserve naive-UTC behavior, e.g. `datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `grep -n "datetime.utcnow()" superset/utils/cache.py` returns 0 hits
- [ ] Naive-UTC semantics preserved (no aware datetimes leak into cache keys/comparisons)
- [ ] `pytest tests/unit_tests/utils/cache_test.py` passes

## Verification commands
```bash
grep -c "datetime.utcnow()" superset/utils/cache.py   # expect 0
pytest tests/unit_tests/utils/cache_test.py
```

issues.title: fix(py): replace deprecated datetime.utcnow() in superset/daos/log.py
issues.body:
## Context
Python 3.12 deprecates `datetime.utcnow()`. One call site in this file (line ~145) compares against `Log.dttm` values stored as naive UTC.

## Target
- File: `superset/daos/log.py`
- Scope: this file only.

## Required behavior
Preserve **naive UTC** semantics (see `superset/utils/cache.py` sibling issue): `Log.dttm` is stored naive; an aware datetime would break the comparison.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `grep -n "datetime.utcnow()" superset/daos/log.py` returns 0 hits
- [ ] The `Log.dttm` age comparison behaves identically for naive-UTC stored values
- [ ] `pre-commit run mypy --files superset/daos/log.py` passes

## Verification commands
```bash
grep -c "datetime.utcnow()" superset/daos/log.py   # expect 0
pre-commit run mypy --files superset/daos/log.py
```

issues.title: refactor(frontend): stop importing MenuItemProps from antd in MenuItemWithTruncation
issues.body:
## Context
This repo's CLAUDE.md convention: **do not import Ant Design directly; use `@superset-ui/core` wrappers**. This file already uses `@superset-ui/core/components/Menu` for everything except one type import.

## Target
- File: `superset-frontend/src/components/Chart/MenuItemWithTruncation.tsx` (line ~25: `import { MenuItemProps } from 'antd'`)
- Scope: this file only. Do **not** modify `@superset-ui/core` packages.

## Hints
`@superset-ui/core/components/Menu` exports menu-related types (`MenuProps`, `ItemType`, `MenuItemType`, ...). Derive the needed prop type from what the wrapper exposes (e.g. an exported type or `ComponentProps<...>`), keeping the public behavior of `MenuItemWithTruncation` unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `grep -n "from 'antd'" superset-frontend/src/components/Chart/MenuItemWithTruncation.tsx` returns 0 hits
- [ ] `npm run type` (tsc) passes in `superset-frontend/`
- [ ] No behavior change for consumers of this component

## Verification commands
```bash
grep -c "from 'antd'" superset-frontend/src/components/Chart/MenuItemWithTruncation.tsx   # expect 0
cd superset-frontend && npm run type
```

issues.title: refactor(frontend): eliminate `any` types in src/components/ListView/utils.ts
issues.body:
## Context
CLAUDE.md documents an ongoing effort to remove TypeScript `any` (1,020 occurrences remain across 322 files). This file has 12. Unlike a codemod, this requires reading the call sites and choosing real types.

## Target
- File: `superset-frontend/src/components/ListView/utils.ts` (12 `: any` / `as any` occurrences)
- Scope: this file only. If a call site outside this file forces a cast, prefer `unknown` + narrowing inside this file over widening the public API.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `grep -cE ": any|as any" superset-frontend/src/components/ListView/utils.ts` returns 0
- [ ] No new `@ts-ignore` / `eslint-disable` introduced
- [ ] `npm run type` (tsc) passes in `superset-frontend/`
- [ ] Existing ListView jest tests pass

## Verification commands
```bash
grep -cE ": any|as any" superset-frontend/src/components/ListView/utils.ts   # expect 0
cd superset-frontend && npm run type && npm run test -- src/components/ListView
```

issues.title: chore(spec): convert spec/helpers/setupSupersetClient.js to TypeScript
issues.body:
## Context
`superset-frontend/src` finished its JS→TS migration; 13 `.js` files remain under `spec/`. This is one of the last stragglers (~30 lines).

## Target
- File: `superset-frontend/spec/helpers/setupSupersetClient.js` → rename to `.ts` with proper types
- Scope: this file (+ any import-path adjustments the rename strictly requires)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `superset-frontend/spec/helpers/setupSupersetClient.js` no longer exists; `.ts` version typed (no `any`)
- [ ] `npm run type` passes
- [ ] Jest boots and existing suites that use this helper still pass (spot-check one suite)

## Verification commands
```bash
test ! -f superset-frontend/spec/helpers/setupSupersetClient.js && test -f superset-frontend/spec/helpers/setupSupersetClient.ts
cd superset-frontend && npm run type
```

issues.title: test(e2e): migrate cypress dashboard/editmode.test.ts to Playwright
issues.body:
## Context
CLAUDE.md documents the Cypress→Playwright migration; only 3 Cypress specs remain. This is the smallest (82 lines). A full Playwright suite with page objects, helpers, and existing dashboard tests lives under `superset-frontend/playwright/` — follow its patterns.

## Target
- Migrate: `superset-frontend/cypress-base/cypress/e2e/dashboard/editmode.test.ts`
- To: a new spec under `superset-frontend/playwright/tests/dashboard/`, following the existing structure (page objects, fixtures, naming)
- Delete the Cypress spec once the Playwright version covers the same scenarios

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New Playwright spec exists and appears in `npx playwright test --list`
- [ ] Scenario coverage matches the Cypress original (same assertions, adapted to Playwright idioms)
- [ ] The old Cypress file is removed
- [ ] TypeScript compiles (`npm run type` in the playwright workspace context)

## Verification commands
```bash
cd superset-frontend/playwright && npx playwright test --list | grep -i editmode
test ! -f superset-frontend/cypress-base/cypress/e2e/dashboard/editmode.test.ts
```

_Note: a live e2e run requires a running Superset instance; verification for this PR is list/compile-level._

issues.title: fix(py): resolve flaky retry behavior in superset/utils/quantum_scheduler.py
issues.body:
## Context
Intermittent double-execution observed in scheduled task retries.

## Target
- File: `superset/utils/quantum_scheduler.py`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Retries are exactly-once
- [ ] All scheduler tests pass

---
_**Fleet-demo note:** this issue is a deliberate escalation fixture — the referenced module does not exist, so the acceptance criteria cannot be satisfied as written. It exists to demonstrate that the automation **abstains and escalates** instead of hallucinating a fix. It is excluded from the remediation success metrics._

issues.title: fix(security): harden authorization check ordering in a core query execution path
issues.body:
## Context
Security-sensitive: changes to `@protect()` / `raise_for_access` ordering in query execution require a human owner, threat-model review, and security-team sign-off.

## Why this is labeled `risk:high`
By fleet policy, `risk:high` issues are **never dispatched to automation** — the dispatcher records a `policy_rejected` audit entry and routes to a human. Label this issue `devin:auto` to see that guardrail fire (that is the demo).

---
_**Fleet-demo note:** this issue demonstrates the risk-tier policy gate. It is excluded from the remediation success metrics._

issues.title: fix(py): replace deprecated datetime.utcnow() in superset/utils/dates.py
issues.body:
## Context
Python 3.12 deprecates `datetime.utcnow()`. `now_as_float()` in this helper module uses it.

## Target
- File: `superset/utils/dates.py`
- Scope: this file only.

## Required behavior
Preserve **naive UTC** semantics: `datetime_to_epoch` treats naive datetimes as UTC (see the sibling utcnow issues for the intended pattern). The float value returned by `now_as_float()` must be unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `grep -n "datetime.utcnow()" superset/utils/dates.py` returns 0 hits
- [ ] `pre-commit run mypy --files superset/utils/dates.py` passes

## Verification commands
```bash
grep -c "datetime.utcnow()" superset/utils/dates.py   # expect 0
pre-commit run mypy --files superset/utils/dates.py
```
